summary: Mixed‑quality soundboard of a historically rich 11/29/66 show – rare gems like Me and My Uncle and Mindbender shine, but the tape is hollow and uneven.
review: The recording captures an early‑Dead set with several rarities (first Me and My Uncle, Mindbender, Lindy, etc.) that make it a must‑have for ’66 heads, yet the audio suffers from tin‑can hollowness, distortion and generational loss. The performance is uneven – Jerry has flashes of brilliance but also repeats licks, Bob’s rhythm work sounds weak, and some songs drag – so the show feels more historic than stellar.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is moderate (rare material but uneven playing) while recording quality is fair to poor; weighted average yields a low‑mid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hollow, tin‑can sound; noticeable distortion and multi‑generation loss; some cuts (Me and My Uncle, Viola Lee) and splices from other dates.

poor_songs: Viola Lee Blues (repetitive licks in places)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The tape actually contains two different shows spliced together, causing a disjointed flow; the "first set" is from 5/19/66 while the remainder is the true 11/29/66 performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Shows flashes of brilliance but also falls into repetitive licks and uneven intensity.
band_member_comments.Bob: His rhythm work sounds pretty bad on this tape.
